config_files.notes: Warframe's single settings file. Stores graphics, audio, and control settings in key=value format. Overwritten by the game on exit — edit only while Warframe is fully closed.

keys.notes: Disabling texture streaming (0) on systems with 16 GB+ RAM and a fast SSD eliminates mid-mission texture pop-in at the cost of longer initial load times. Recommended for high-spec systems.

Warframe's combat involves extreme particle density from Warframe abilities. Disruption, Arbitration, and Steel Path survival missions with multiple ability-spamming Warframes can cause significant frame drops at High particle quality.
DX11 should always be enabled on modern hardware. DX10 mode exists as a fallback for very old GPUs.

keys.effect: Target refresh rate / frame rate cap in Hz.
keys.notes: Warframe's EE.cfg frame cap aligns with monitor refresh rate by default. Setting this explicitly prevents the game from running at incorrect rates after a monitor change.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on RTX hardware. FSR Quality on AMD. Warframe added DLSS support in 2022 and FSR in 2021.
Disable unless playing on low-end hardware. Dynamic resolution causes inconsistent visual quality during ability spam — the framebuffer resolution changes mid-combat.
Off. DoF during ability casts and heavy particle scenes reduces visual clarity and has measurable GPU cost.
Off. Warframe's fast movement (bullet jumping, void dashing) makes motion blur a significant source of visual noise.

notes: Warframe is a free-to-play game available via Steam, Epic, and standalone launcher. All versions share the same EE.cfg path.
Warframe's Evolution Engine has been continuously updated since 2013. The game uses DX11 on PC and supports Vulkan on some platforms (not PC as of April 2026).
Warframe ability spam creates extreme particle density in late-game content. Particle quality is the most impactful graphics setting for endgame (Steel Path, Arbitrations, Netracells) performance.
The game supports clan and alliance multiplayer, but instances are limited to 4 players (squad) or 50 players (Relay hubs). Relay hubs are not gameplay areas and have their own lightweight renderer.
EE.cfg is rewritten on every game exit. Always edit with the game fully closed and verify changes were not overwritten on next launch.
